preview_text:
The model favors a USA win at 36%, but the margin over Paraguay is narrow. Paraguay's 33% win probability places them just 3 percentage points behind, while a draw sits at 32% — suggesting all three outcomes remain genuinely competitive on the model's assessment.

The spread across win/draw/loss outcomes reflects a close contest with meaningful uncertainty. The 3-point gap between USA and Paraguay win odds indicates no clear favorite in strictly probabilistic terms. A draw at 32% is plausible enough to feature prominently in match expectations, signaling that neither team is projected to dominate decisively.

The sportsbook consensus diverges notably from the model. Books assign the USA a 48% win probability — a 12-point premium over the model's 36%. Conversely, Paraguay trades at 25% in the market versus 33% on the model, a 8-point gap. The draw splits the difference less dramatically (27% books vs. 32% model). No Polymarket data is available yet. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these divergent readings.
